summary:
Radiolabeled albumin is uniquely suited to determine interstitial fluid volume because it uniformly distributes between plasma and interstitial fluids, allowing precise estimation using indicator-dilution methods based on measured concentrations over time. This distinguishes it from other tracers that cannot effectively separate plasma and interstitial compartment measurements.  

\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
